methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Long Wall is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Dan Piemont and Harry O'Hanley. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed). This peer set spans $60M to $500M in valuation across the Defense & Security sector.

The peer-median method, the sole applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields a median implied valuation of $225M. The median reflects the midpoint of four comparable transactions and accounts for stage heterogeneity within the peer cohort—three Series A companies and one seed-stage peer. No revenue multiples, DCF, or precedent transaction methods were applicable due to data constraints.

The triangulated valuation range is $191M to $259M, with a median of $225M. This range assumes peer comparability holds despite Long Wall's seed status relative to three Series A peers in the sample. Key uncertainties include the limited sample size (n=4), stage-to-stage variance in risk profiles between seed and Series A, and unobserved differences in technology maturity or market traction across peers. A broader dataset including additional seed-stage defense companies or revenue-based multiples would strengthen conviction in this range.
